a: This class provides information about an album in the iTunes library.
This class represents an artist, such as the performer of a song.
This class represents the artwork for a media item.
These constants specify the possible formats of the data that [imageDataFormat](/documentation/ituneslibrary/itlibartwork/imagedataformat) returns.
The image data corresponds to a bitmap image.
The image data corresponds to a GIF image.
The image data corresponds to a JPEG image.
The image data corresponds to a JPEG2000 image.
The image data corresponds to a PICT image.
The image data corresponds to a PNG image.
The image data corresponds to a TIFF image.
The image data corresponds to a bitmap image.
The image data format is unknown.
These constants specify the possible kinds of distinguished playlists.
The default Smart Playlist that iTunes creates of the user’s music from the 1990s.
The playlist contains all the user’s iOS apps in the iTunes library.
The playlist contains all the audiobooks in the iTunes library.
The default Smart Playlist that iTunes creates of the user’s classical music.
The playlist contains all the user’s home videos in the iTunes library.
The playlist contains all the music videos in the iTunes library.
The playlist contains all the user’s loved music.
The playlist contains all the movies in the iTunes library.
The playlist contains all the music items in the iTunes library.
The default Smart Playlist that iTunes creates of the user’s music shows and movies.
The default Smart Playlist that iTunes creates of the user’s music videos.
The default Smart Playlist that iTunes creates of the user’s top rated media items.
The playlist isn’t a distinguished playlist.
The playlist contains all the podcasts in the iTunes library.
The playlist contains all the user’s purchases from the iTunes Store.
The default Smart Playlist that iTunes creates of the user’s most recently added media items.
The default Smart Playlist that iTunes creates of the user’s most recently played media items.
The playlist contains all the iOS ringtones in the iTunes library.
The playlist contains all the TV shows in the iTunes library.
The default Smart Playlist that iTunes creates of the user’s 25 most played media items.
The playlist contains all the voice memos in the iTunes library.
The playlist contains all the user’s iTunes U items.
These constants describe the features that an iTunes library supports.
The iTunes library doesn’t support any export features.
These constants describe initialization options for an iTunes library.
iTunes library data loads upon request, rather than during initialization.
No initialization options apply.
This class describes a media entity, which can be a media item, such as an audio track.
The unique identifier of the media entity.
This class describes a media item (a track) in the iTunes library, such as a song, a video, or a podcast.
These constants specify the location type of a media item.
The media item location refers to a URL, such as a podcast.
The media item location refers to a local file.
The media item location refers to a remote file.
The media item location type is unknown.
These constants specify the possible ratings of media item lyrics.
The media item lyrics don’t contain explicit language.
The media item lyrics contain explicit language.
There is no rating information for the media item lyrics.
These constants specify the possible media kinds of a media item.
The media item is an audio tone that’s not a protected ringtone on an iOS device.
The media item is an audiobook.
The media item is an EPUB file or an iBooks Author book.
The media item is an iTunes Extra or an iTunes LP item.
The media item is a non-iTunes Store movie.
The media item is an iOS app.
The media item is a QuickTime movie with embedded Flash.
The media item is a movie.
The media item is a music video.
The media item is a PDF file that iTunes treats as a book unless the user overrides it.
The media item is an unwrapped PDF file that’s part of a music album.
The media item is an audio or a video podcast.
The media item is an iOS ringtone.
The media item is a song.
The media item is a TV show.
The media item kind is unknown.
The media item is a recorded voice memo.
The media item is an iTunes U audio or video file.
These constants specify the play status of the media item.
The user has played the media item or iTunes doesn’t track the play status for this kind of media item.
The user has partially played the media item.
The user hasn’t played the media item.
The date and time the user added the media item to the iTunes library.
The name of the artist that iTunes associates with the media item’s album.
The number of discs in the media item’s album.
The disc number in the media item’s album.
This property indicates whether the album of the media item is a compilation.
This property indicates whether the media item’s album is gapless.
The rating of the media item’s album.
This property indicates whether iTunes computes the rating of the media item’s album from the ratings of individual tracks in the album.
The title of the media item’s album.
The track count of the media item’s album.
The name of the artist that iTunes associates with the media item.
The artwork for the media item.
The beats-per-minute (BPM) of the media item.
The bitrate of the media item in kbit/s.
The podcast category of the media item, if the media item is a podcast.
Any comments that iTunes associates with the media item.
The name of the composer that iTunes associates with the media item.
The extended content rating of the media item.
A podcast description of the media item, if the media item is a podcast.
The size in bytes of the media item on disk.
The file type of the media item.
The genre that iTunes associates with the media item.
The grouping of the media item.
This property indicates whether the media item has artwork.
This property indicates whether the media item has digital rights management (DRM) protection.
This property indicates whether the media item is a purchased media item.
This property indicates whether the user disabled the media item.
This property indicates whether the media item is a video media item, such as a video podcast or movie.
The kind of media item file, such as an MPEG audio file.
The date and time the user last played the media item in iTunes, or `nil` if the user hasn’t played the media item.
The location of the media item on disk.
The type of the media item with respect to its location.
The content rating of the media item’s lyrics.
The media kind of the media item.
The date and time that iTunes last modified the media item.
The number of times the user has played the media item in iTunes.
The play status of the media item.
The rating of the media item.
This property indicates whether iTunes computes the media item’s rating.
The release date of the media item.
The sample rate of the media item in samples-per-second.
The size in bytes of the media item on disk.
The date and time that the user last skipped the media item.
The name of the artist that iTunes associates with the media item’s album, for use when sorting.
The title of the media item’s album, for use when sorting.
The name of the media item’s artist, for use when sorting.
The name of the composer that iTunes associates with the media item, for use when sorting.
The title of the media item to use when sorting.
If nonzero, the actual time that playback for the media item starts instead of 0:00 (in milliseconds).
If nonzero, the actual time that playback for the media item stops versus the total time (in milliseconds).
The title of the media item.
The length of the media item in milliseconds.
The numerical position of the media item within its album.
The number of times that the user skipped the media item.
The name of the episode, if the media item is an episode of a TV series.
The order of the episode, if the media item is an episode of a TV series.
The height in pixels, if the media item is a video.
This property indicates whether a video media item is high-definition.
The corresponding TV season, if the media item is an episode of a TV series.
The name of the corresponding TV series, if the media item is an episode in a TV series.
The sorting name of the corresponding TV series, if the media item is an episode in a TV series.
The width in pixels, if the media item is a video.
The voice-over language of the media item.
The volume adjustment for the media item, if any.
The volume normalization energy that iTunes applies to the media item to bring the average or peak amplitude to a target level.
The release year of the media item.
This class encapsulates the video information of a video media item.
This class describes a playlist in the iTunes library.
These constants specify the possible kinds of playlists.
A playlist folder that the user or iTunes creates, such as *My Playlist Folder* or *Genius Mixes*.
A playlist iTunes creates of songs that go well with a song the user specifies.
An ongoing playlist in a particular genre—like a commercial-free radio station playing the user’s favorite songs—that iTunes creates from music in the user’s iTunes library.
A standard playlist that the user or iTunes creates, such as *Music*, *Movies*, *Pop Mix*, or *My Awesome Playlist*.
A playlist with contents that iTunes generates by evaluating a set of rules, such as *90s Music* or *Songs from 1999*.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the API exposes every item in the playlist.
An indication of whether the playlist has a special distinction.
The media items (tracks) in the playlist.
An indication of the type of playlist.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the playlist represents the entire iTunes library.
The name or title of the playlist.
The unique persistent identifier of the playlist’s parent playlist.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the playlist is the primary playlist.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the playlist is visible to the user in iTunes.
This class serves as the entry point to the iTunesLibrary framework.
This notification is sent to NSDistributedNotificationCenter when a change has occurred in the library.
The date and time that the user added the media item to the iTunes library.
The album of the media item.
The name of the artist iTunes associates with the album.
All the media items (tracks) in the iTunes library.
All the playlists in the iTunes library.
The major version number of the API the iTunesLibrary framework exposes.
The minor version number of the API the iTunesLibrary framework exposes.
The version of iTunes that created or modified the iTunes library you’re accessing.
The artist iTunes associates with the album.
Information about the artist that iTunes associates with the media item.
The artwork of the media item.
Retrieves the artwork from a media file that may or may not be in the iTunes library.
The beats-per-minute (BPM) of the media item.
The bitrate of the media item in kbit/s.
The podcast category of the media item, if the media item is a podcast.
Any comments that iTunes associates with the media item.
The content rating of the media item.
The description of the media item, if it’s a podcast.
The number of discs in a multiple-disc set.
The index (1, 2, 3, and so on) of the disc within an album that’s a multiple-disc set.
An indication of whether the playlist has a special distinction.
Executes a provided block with the fetched values for the item properties.
Executes a provided block with the fetched values for all properties in the entity except for the provided set.
The name of the episode, if the media item is an episode of a TV series.
The numerical order of the episode, if the media item is an episode of a TV series.
A bitwise OR combination of the features of this library.
The size in bytes of this media item on disk.
The file type of the media item.
The genre of the media item, if any.
The grouping of the media item that the user specifies or that iTunes specifies in the file’s metadata.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the media item has artwork.
Retrieve the properties of the media in the user’s iTunes library.
The artwork image.
The raw image data of the artwork in the format that [imageDataFormat](/documentation/ituneslibrary/itlibartwork/imagedataformat) specifies.
The format of the artwork image data that [imageData](/documentation/ituneslibrary/itlibartwork/imagedata) returns.
Initializes an instance of [ITLibrary](/documentation/ituneslibrary/itlibrary) that can retrieve media entities.
Initializes an instance of `ITLibrary` that can retrieve media entities.
Indicates whether the API exposes every item in the playlist.
A Boolean value that indicates whether this media item is an iTunes Match or an iTunes in the Cloud item.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the album is a compilation.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the media item has digital rights management (DRM) protection.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the album is gapless.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the video is high-definition.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the playlist represents the entire iTunes library.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the playlist is the primary playlist.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the user purchased the media item from the iTunes Store.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the system computes the rating of the album using the ratings of individual tracks in the album.
A Boolean value that indicates whether iTunes computes the media item’s rating from its album rating.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the user disabled the media item.
A Boolean value that indicates whether this media item is a video, such as a TV show, video podcast, or movie.
A Boolean value that indicates whether the playlist is visible to the user in iTunes.
The media items (tracks) in the playlist.
The kind of media item file, such as an MPEG audio file.
An indication of the type of playlist.
The playlist contains all the audiobooks in the iTunes library.
The date and time the user last played the media item, or `nil` if the user hasn’t played it.
Creates and initializes an instance of `ITLibrary` that can retrieve media entities.
Creates and initializes an instance of ITLibrary that can retrieve media entities.
The location of the media item on disk.
The type of the media item with respect to its location.
The content rating of the media item’s lyrics.
The location of the iTunes music folder.
The kind of media item.
The date and time that iTunes last modified the media item.
The location of the iTunes music folder.
The name of the artist.

The unique persistent identifier of the playlist’s parent playlist.

The play status for the media.
The rating of the album.
The rating of the media item.
The release date of the media item.
Refreshes the data that the framework uses.

The name of the corresponding TV series, if the media item is an episode in a TV series.
A Boolean value indicating whether to show content rating labels.
The size in bytes of the media item on disk.

The date and time that the user last skipped the media item.
The name to use when sorting by album artist.
The name to use when sorting by composer.
The name of the artist to use when sorting.
The sorting name of the corresponding TV series, if the media item is an episode in a TV series.
The title to use when sorting by album title.
The title of the media item to use when sorting.

If nonzero, the actual time playback of the media item stops versus the total time (in milliseconds).

The title of the media item.

Gets the value for a specified media property key.
The height of the video in pixels.
Video information (such as width and height) about the media item, if it’s a video.
